name: Glitchcore Porcelain
rationale: Fuses the uncanny-valley dread of cracked-doll aesthetics with Y2K glitch-art corruption artifacts — the mirror hall renders in pristine pastel vaporwave gradients until frame thirteen, where JPEG decay, scanline tears, and chromatic-aberration bleeds fracture the surface like a corrupted memory file, making the mask-crack moment feel like a system crash.
preamble: Digital mixed-media glitch art: pristine porcelain-smooth pastel gradients (blush, mint, pale gold) abruptly corrupted by JPEG compression artifacts, scanline tears, and violent chromatic aberration bleeds in magenta and electric cyan; linework is clean vector-crisp in the stable zones and dissolves into datamoshed pixel smears at the fracture points; lighting is eerie bioluminescent glow from within the portal face; mood is serene-turned-haunted, like a vintage Windows screensaver mid-crash; references: Yung Jake glitch portraiture, Beeple early-era digital surrealism, vaporwave aesthetic gone wrong, corrupted VHS still-frame.
